meta_title: For Founders: testing automation to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to testing automation and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Test Automation to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use test automation to overcome a lack of portfolio. A professional guide for founders leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code to ship fast.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a myth; founders solve it by demonstrating reliability. Integrate automated testing using Antigravity and Claude Code to build a high-velocity, bug-free MVP. By showcasing your test coverage and architecture, you prove technical maturity to investors, effectively replacing a legacy portfolio with a verified, production-ready product.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the founder's greatest enemy, but the 'no portfolio' stigma is the silent killer of early-stage momentum. You have the vision, but your lack of a track record makes every stakeholder hesitate. They don't need to see what you built in 2018; they need to see that the code you are writing right now is robust, scalable, and engineered to survive the chaos of a market launch.

We stop the guesswork here. By leveraging automated testing as your primary growth lever, you transform your development process into a product feature. This isn't just about catching bugs; it's about building a 'trust-first' architecture that signals to users and investors that your platform is a professional-grade asset, not a weekend experiment.

steps.title: Architecting the Test Suite
steps.description: Define the critical paths of your SaaS. Focus on user authentication and database transactions to ensure foundational stability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a testing strategy document that outlines unit and integration tests for a SaaS authentication flow, prioritizing security and edge-case handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a test/auth.test.ts file using Vitest for user login and registration flows'.

steps.title: Automating API Validation
steps.description: Use automated requests to verify your backend responses against your schema, ensuring data integrity before you even launch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a script that validates API response schemas for all critical CRUD operations in an Express.js environment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate integration tests for the /api/projects endpoint to verify data payload structure'.

steps.title: Simulating User Journeys
steps.description: Implement end-to-end tests that mimic real-world usage patterns to prove your features work under load.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a Playwright configuration that simulates a full user sign-up to dashboard flow, including database state verification.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Setup Playwright for e2e testing and create a spec file for the onboarding user journey'.

steps.title: Continuous Integration Hookup
steps.description: Automate your testing pipeline so every commit is verified, turning your repository into a living proof of quality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the full test suite and reports coverage metrics on every pull request.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a .github/workflows/test.yml file to automate testing on push and pull requests'.

steps.title: Visualizing Test Coverage
steps.description: Generate a coverage report to showcase your codebase's health to potential stakeholders.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a reporting tool to output a clean, visual representation of current test coverage for documentation purposes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure vitest coverage to output in HTML format and add a script to package.json'.

pro_tips: Treat your test suite as your 'Technical Resume'; a clean, high-coverage repository is more impressive to CTOs than a list of side projects.
Focus on 'happy path' tests first—if the primary revenue flow is broken, no amount of edge-case testing will save your MRR.
Use Claude Code to refactor your tests into modular files; keep your test logic as clean as your production code to avoid technical debt.
Automate your documentation alongside your tests; if a feature isn't tested and documented, it doesn't exist in the eyes of an enterprise client.
Run tests in parallel to keep CI times under 3 minutes; speed is the ultimate metric for developer productivity.
content.next_steps: Once your test suite is live, deploy your project to a staging environment and use the coverage reports as a marketing asset in your pitch deck to demonstrate professional-grade engineering rigor.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer an amateur; you are a builder with a verified, test-driven product. The market rewards those who ship with confidence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
